metodologiaPage.metaTitle: Propyte Methodology: SOP-3.2 Scorecard — How we validate a development
metodologiaPage.metaDescription: The documented process we apply to every development before listing it: 5 criteria, legal and commercial team, auditable decision.
metodologiaPage.heroTitle: SOP-3.2 Scorecard: how we validate a development before listing it
metodologiaPage.heroLead: Every development in the Propyte portfolio goes through a documented strategic validation with five mandatory criteria. If one criterion fails, the development does not enter. This page summarizes how the process works.
metodologiaPage.section1Lead: Each criterion has a documented rubric with verifiable sub-points. The review is signed by one member of the legal team and one of the commercial team.
metodologiaPage.criterion1Body: We verify the master deed, trust status (when applicable), legal incorporation of the developer, current tax registration and anti-corruption review. If the deed is unclear or active litigation exists, the development is not listed.
metodologiaPage.criterion2Body: We review the developer's track record of previous deliveries, purchase contracts and documented guarantees. If there is no verifiable track record or the contract does not protect the buyer against delays, an addendum is required before listing.
metodologiaPage.criterion3Body: We compare the per-square-meter price against the zone average, adjusting for amenities and stage (presale/construction/delivery). If the price falls outside the range without clear justification, it is documented and communicated to the client before the sale.
metodologiaPage.criterion4Body: Site visit (or photographically documented progress), review of specifications, material quality and validation of committed progress. If there is a gap between what was committed and delivered, correction is required before continuing.
metodologiaPage.criterion5Body: Real demand analysis by zone, expected occupancy (short and long term), typical buyer profile and resale potential. We do not promise ROI without methodology — we only discuss honest ranges with available data.
metodologiaPage.section2Title: What happens if a criterion fails
metodologiaPage.section2Body: The development is not published in the portfolio. If the developer can remedy (e.g. contractual addendum, additional evidence), the review reopens. If it cannot be remedied, the development stays out. We do not make exceptions for commercial pressure.
metodologiaPage.section3Body: Every SOP-3.2 Scorecard carries the signature of one legal team member and one commercial team member. The validation is internally auditable and can be shared with the client upon request.
metodologiaPage.ctaBody: Talk to a Propyte advisor and request to see the Scorecard for the development that interests you.

avisoLegalPage.metaTitle: Investment Legal Notice: disclaimer, simulator terms and sources
avisoLegalPage.metaDescription: Full YMYL disclaimer from Propyte: scope of the financial simulator, assumptions, legal limitations and cited sources on propyte.com.
avisoLegalPage.heroLead: This notice applies to all figures, projections, simulations and ranges published on propyte.com. Any investment decision is the user's responsibility and should be consulted with a Propyte advisor and, when applicable, with an independent legal or financial professional.
avisoLegalPage.section1Body: Projections, simulations and return ranges shown on the site are estimates based on historical market data, public comparables and user assumptions. They do not constitute a guarantee of returns or financial recommendation. Past returns do not guarantee future results.
avisoLegalPage.section2Body: The financial simulator allows projecting estimated ROI, down payment, monthly payments and appreciation based on parameters the user defines: price, down payment, term, appreciation rate, rental scenario. Results are referential. They do not include acquisition tax, notary fees, bank trust (when applicable), management commissions or insurance, unless the user explicitly includes them.
avisoLegalPage.section3Body: When we publish aggregate market figures (ROI, appreciation, demand, etc.), we cite the source and the update date. As of this version, Propyte does not hold active commercial licenses with Softec or AMPI Q.Roo — published ranges are based on internal comparables and public data. When commercial licensing exists, sources will be updated with the report name and publication date.
avisoLegalPage.section4Body: The Quintana Roo coastal area is in a federal restricted zone. Foreigners purchase property through a 50-year bank trust (renewable). Propyte facilitates the process but is not a notary or banking institution. Specific costs and procedures are confirmed by the notary and the assigned trustee institution.
avisoLegalPage.section5Body: Every development in the Propyte portfolio goes through the SOP-3.2 Scorecard (see /metodologia). The validation is documented and internally auditable. It does not constitute a guarantee of the development's future performance nor does it ensure specific availability, pricing or sale terms — those terms are set by the developer in their contract.
avisoLegalPage.section6Body: Propyte operates as a real estate marketplace and commercial advisor. We do not guarantee specific returns, appreciation or occupancy. Data is subject to update. The final purchase decision and verification of information are the responsibility of the buyer and the professionals supporting them (notary, accountant, legal advisor).
avisoLegalPage.section7Body: If you detect inaccurate information, a figure without a source or a missing disclaimer, write to info@propyte.com with subject "Compliance". We review every report and publish a correction if applicable.

homeFAQ.title: What investors ask us the most
homeFAQ.q1: Can a foreigner buy property in Tulum or Playa del Carmen?
homeFAQ.a1: Yes. Quintana Roo's coast sits in the federal restricted zone, so a foreigner buys through a 50-year bank trust (fideicomiso, renewable). It's a standard process and a Propyte advisor walks you through every step.
homeFAQ.q2: How much does the legal closing of a property in Riviera Maya cost?
homeFAQ.a2: Closing costs usually range from 6% to 8% of the property value and include acquisition tax, notary, trust (if applicable) and permits. Figures vary by state and property type — we ask the advisor for a personalized analysis.
homeFAQ.q3: What is a realistic ROI for an Airbnb in Tulum?
homeFAQ.a3: ROI depends on zone, product quality, management and seasonality. We do not publish point figures without methodology — we prefer to discuss honest ranges with an advisor who knows your specific investment and current comparables.
homeFAQ.q4: What happens if I buy presale and the development is delayed?
homeFAQ.a4: The contract must specify penalties for delays and exit options. We validate this under the Schedule Reliability criterion before listing the development. If you buy and a delay occurs, your contract is your documented protection.

developers.heroTitle: We commercialize your development from start to finish.
developers.heroSubtitle: And "start" begins earlier than you’d think: we help you read what the market wants, evaluate the land, and conceptualize the right product. From there we build the brand, generate demand, sell, collect, close title, and hand over. One partner for the whole cycle.
developers.problemTitle: You run the business. We handle everything else that makes it sell.
developers.problemIntro: Most boutique developments don’t stumble on the bricks — they stumble on the market decisions surrounding the project:
developers.problem1Desc: Choosing unit types, amenities, and pricing without knowing what the buyer in that zone actually wants. The most expensive mistake, because it’s made before you start.
developers.problem2Title: Selling in a market you don’t command.
developers.problem2Desc: You know your business, but not necessarily the buyer — where they come from, or what moves them to close.
developers.problem3Title: Marketing on one side, sales on the other.
developers.problem3Desc: One agency runs campaigns, another team handles leads, and no one owns the final number. Spend that dilutes across vendors.
developers.problem4Desc: Branding, site, CRM, sales force, notary, collections, handover. Each with its own contract, pace, and invoice.
developers.problem5Title: Closing the sale, then learning the hard part starts there.
developers.problem5Desc: Collections, title, and handover are where a development with no back-office gets stuck — right after the buyer has paid.
developers.cycleTitle: The full cycle, in one hand
developers.cycleIntro: What a developer would have to assemble from several agencies and in-house teams, Propyte already has running. Four blocks that work as one:
developers.cycle1Tag: Market intelligence · from the ground up
developers.cycle1Desc: We come in early: we help you evaluate the land and conceptualize the product around what the market wants — which unit types, what pricing, which mix has the most demand in that zone. The same intelligence that later backs every commercial decision. Every step, on verifiable data.
developers.cycle2Desc: Naming, identity, development website, social, content, and campaigns. The full machinery to attract the right buyer — without hiring a separate agency.
developers.cycle3Desc: Launch strategy, sales team, dynamic pricing, lead generation, CRM, and real-time reporting. You always know what’s happening with your development.
developers.cycle4Tag: Post-sale closing · what almost no one gives you
developers.cycle4Desc: Collections, payment-plan administration, title, and unit handover. The stretch where a development with no back-office gets stuck — and the one we already operate from our own structure.
developers.cycleCallout: Most brokers sell and leave. We close the cycle: from first render to handing over the keys, without you coordinating notaries or collections.
developers.productsTitle: Four ways to work with us
developers.productsIntro: You choose how much of the cycle you delegate. Three are mandates where Propyte runs the commercialization; the fourth adds our capabilities to a sale you already have underway.
developers.productSupportTagline: Capabilities on top of your commercialization.
developers.productSupportDesc: For the development already selling —with your team or another broker— that wants to add Propyte’s sales force, technology, and content without switching horses midstream. Includes: advisors, CRM, lead generation, social and content, reporting.
developers.productLaunchTagline: Your inventory to market, without building a team.
developers.productLaunchDesc: For those who need to solve the sale with a solid brand and demand engine. Adds: market intelligence, development branding and website, and full commercialization with team, CRM, and transparent reporting.
developers.productEngineDesc: For those who want the entire commercial operation, not just the closing. Adds: dedicated sales team, dynamic pricing, collections and payment-plan administration, with a full market study as the base.
developers.productTurnkeyTagline: From launch to handover. You steer the business; we run the whole cycle.
developers.productTurnkeyDesc: For the developer of one to three towers who wants to delegate the entire commercial and closing operation. The highest level of delegation. Includes the full cycle: brand and demand, commercialization with a dedicated team, dynamic pricing, collections, title, handover, and continuous market monitoring throughout the mandate.
developers.productsChooseBody: If your development is already selling and you just want to reinforce it, it’s Support. If you need Propyte to run the commercialization, you choose between Launch, Engine, and PRO·360 based on how much of the cycle you’d rather delegate. In your development’s proposal we define together which one fits your project, your team, and your market.
developers.tableFootnote: Paid campaign spend, when it applies, is approved and billed separately. The commercialization commission is defined in your development’s specific proposal — it depends on your market and your project’s potential.

developers.studyTitle: We start with data, not promises
developers.studyBody1: Every commercialization with Propyte starts with a market study of your development. It’s not a formality: it’s the document that defines what each unit type sells for, which product mix has the most demand, how your competition behaves, and what return your buyer can expect. It’s the foundation of the entire strategy — and the proof that when we propose something, we back it with data.
developers.studyBody2: The study has a cost, but it works as an advance: if you decide to commercialize with Propyte, that amount is credited against our commission as the first units sell. And if you decide not to continue, you keep a bankable market analysis — the kind a bank would accept as credit support — that’s yours.
developers.studyCallout: Before we ask you to trust us with your development, we show you how we read the market. The study is our calling card: a rigorous analysis, with verifiable sources, that serves you whether or not you work with us.
developers.processTitle: From the first conversation to handing over the keys
developers.step1Desc: We analyze your development, your market, and your competition. We come out with pricing by unit type and a data-backed strategy — not hunches.
developers.step2Desc: We present the product that best fits your project (Support, Launch, Engine, or Turnkey) with clear scope and terms. No fine print.
developers.step3Desc: We set up whatever’s missing —brand, site, team, campaigns— and start commercialization with live reporting from day one.
developers.step4Desc: We sell, collect, close title, and hand over. You see every number in real time; we operate the rest.
developers.faq1Q: What types of developments do you commercialize?
developers.faq1A: Vertical residential (apartments, penthouses), horizontal residential (houses, townhouses), lots, and mixed-use projects across Quintana Roo and Yucatán.
developers.faq2A: We operate on a sales commission, not a fixed monthly fee. The commission is defined in your development’s proposal, because it depends on your market and your project’s potential. Every commercialization starts with a market study that works as an advance: if you move forward with us, it’s credited against the commission.
developers.faq3Q: Which regions do you operate in?
developers.faq3A: The coastal Riviera Maya and Yucatán: Playa del Carmen, Tulum, Cancún, and Mérida.
developers.faq4Q: How long does it take to see results?
developers.faq4A: The study and proposal take a few weeks. Sales velocity depends on your product, your pricing, and your market — which is exactly why we don’t promise a number before studying it. What we do guarantee from day one is real-time reporting: you see actual progress, not an optimistic projection.
developers.faq5A: It depends on the product. With Support we add our capabilities to a sale you already have underway, even if you work with another broker. With the mandates (Launch, Engine, Turnkey) we operate the full cycle, which does imply an exclusive relationship — we define it in the proposal.
developers.faq6A: A CRM with pipeline, a real-time reporting dashboard so you see sales, collections, and title progress with no black boxes, and continuous market monitoring in the products that include it. The technology is there to give you full visibility, not to impress.
developers.faq7Q: Who is my point of contact?
developers.faq7A: A dedicated Account Manager for your development, coordinating all four blocks (intelligence, brand, commercialization, and closing) so you deal with one person, not six vendors.
developers.faq8Q: Can I cancel at any time?
developers.faq8A: The initial study commits you to nothing — if you don’t continue, you keep a bankable analysis that’s yours. The terms of each mandate are set out clearly in the proposal, no fine print.

brokers.heroSubtitle: Join our network of allied brokers: vetted inventory kept current, launches and promotions before anyone else, a Propyte advisor backing you with your clients, and clear commission agreements. You keep your independence and your client relationship; we bring the product and the support.
brokers.heroMicroLine: Looking to grow inside Propyte as a career advisor? That’s something else →
brokers.heroWaText: Hi, I'm interested in Propyte's broker program
brokers.bridgeTitle: How we work, clear from the start:
brokers.bridgeBody: you bring your client base; we bring the vetted product, the support, and the closing. We don't generate leads for you — we amplify what you already bring. If you have clients who trust you, this is for you.
brokers.whyTitle: Everything you need to close more, without losing your independence
brokers.why1Desc: Access to Propyte's active developments with up-to-date prices, floor plans, and availability. Inventory that already passed our validation — not unfiltered listings.
brokers.why2Desc: When your client asks about fideicomiso, ROI, or legal matters, you're not alone: a Propyte advisor supports you with the technical and market knowledge to close on solid ground.
brokers.why3Desc: You register your client and they stay associated with you. Your relationship is respected — we work to help you close, not to bypass you.
brokers.why4Desc: Commission defined in a written agreement before you start, depending on the development. No surprises, no fine print.
brokers.why5Desc: We notify you of new inventory, pre-sales, and promotions before they go public. Market information so you arrive with an edge.
brokers.why6Desc: Brochures, renders, and content per development, plus product training so you master what you sell.
brokers.modelsTitle: Two ways to work with our inventory
brokers.modelsIntro: Depending on the development, we work with you under one of two models. In both, your commission is set in a written agreement before you start.
brokers.model1Desc: On developments we commercialize as exclusive MasterBroker, you earn a percentage of the sale you close. Defined per development, clear and in writing. Open to all our allied brokers.
brokers.model1PointsIntro: What you gain selling our exclusive:
brokers.model1Point1Desc: Priority access to pre-launches and promotions on our exclusives, so you reach your client with proposals ahead of the open market.
brokers.model1Point2Desc: Brochures, renders, video, and spec sheets per development, so you present with professional materials without having to produce them.
brokers.model1Point3Desc: The official price list and up-to-date availability from the exclusive development, so you never misquote or offer something already sold.
brokers.model1Point4Title: A direct line to the project expert.
brokers.model1Point4Desc: We connect you with the Propyte advisor who knows that development inside out, to resolve your client's questions with no middlemen.
brokers.model2Desc: On developments we work under brokerage, we split the final commission 50/50 with you. Designed for the broker who brings a client from another city or country: you bring the relationship, we bring the local ground your client needs.
brokers.model2PointsIntro: We work as a team: you bring the half only you have, we bring ours. That 50/50 rests on three things:
brokers.model2Point1Desc: We work with prior commission agreements and a direct relationship with the developer. Your payout doesn't hinge on closing a deal with a third party you don't know — we aim to back it in advance.
brokers.model2Point2Title: Your client's risk, covered by both of us.
brokers.model2Point2Desc: You know your client and have their trust; we know the ground, the developer, and every detail of the zone. Together we cover the two halves a sound investment needs: the relationship and the local context.
brokers.model2Point3Desc: We accompany your client on tours of the proposals and developments, with a vehicle and a Propyte advisor. And if you can't join the tour with them, we handle it anyway — without affecting your commission.
brokers.modelsCallout: Are you a local broker? Model 1 is yours on every exclusive development. The 50/50 brokerage is built for those working remotely who need a team on the ground.
brokers.processTitle: From application to first sale, in 4 steps
brokers.step1Desc: Fill out the form and tell us about your experience and your business.
brokers.step2Desc: Welcome call with your Account Manager. We activate your account and sign the commission agreement.
brokers.step3Desc: You get access to the vetted portfolio, per-development materials, and your backing Propyte advisor.
brokers.step4Desc: You register your clients —protected under your name— and share the properties. We back you all the way to closing.
brokers.platformSubtitle: A platform to manage inventory, clients, and commissions in one place — no scattered spreadsheets or WhatsApp threads. Coming in the next few months; in the meantime, your Account Manager gives you everything you need.
brokers.platformFeature1Desc: Availability and pricing kept current, by development.
brokers.platformFeature2Desc: Your clients and their stage, on a clear board.
brokers.platformFeature3Desc: What you've earned and what's pending.
brokers.platformFeature4Desc: Materials, sheets, and follow-up in one place.
brokers.faqTitle: What brokers ask us before joining
brokers.faq1Q: Do I need to live in the Riviera Maya to join?
brokers.faq1A: No. We work with brokers across Mexico —Mexico City, Monterrey, Guadalajara, Querétaro, Mérida, Cancún, Playa del Carmen, Tulum— and with international allies in the United States, Canada, and Latin America. What matters is your client base, not your city: many of our best brokers work with buyers investing in the area from their home country.
brokers.faq2Q: How does the commission structure work?
brokers.faq2A: It depends on the development: on exclusive ones you earn a percentage of the sale; on brokerage ones (for out-of-town brokers) we share the commission 50/50. In both cases it’s set in a written agreement before you start. See the "How you earn" section above for details.
brokers.faq3Q: Can my agents work with Propyte?
brokers.faq3A: Yes. You register your agents and we connect them directly with your Propyte advisor, so everyone has direct communication on inventory, clients, and progress. You keep visibility of your team; they get a direct line to the person backing them.
brokers.faq4Q: What inventory do you have available?
brokers.faq4A: Vetted developments in pre-sale and move-in ready across the Riviera Maya (Playa del Carmen, Tulum, Cancún) and Yucatán (Mérida). Each development passed our validation before entering the portfolio.
brokers.faq5Q: When and how do I get paid?
brokers.faq5A: Once all conditions of the deal are met, your commission is paid out within 7 to 15 business days.
brokers.faq6Q: Can I bring my own clients?
brokers.faq6A: That's exactly how we work. You bring and register your clients, and they're protected under your name. We don't generate leads for you — you bring your book and we bring the vetted product, the backing of a Propyte advisor, and the closing. Your client relationship is always yours.
brokers.faq7Q: Do you offer training for my team?
brokers.faq7A: Yes. Product training per development and sales-process training, for you and your team, so you master what you sell and how to close it.

about.label: About Us
about.title: Meet Propyte
about.subtitle: Property + Byte. Modernizing real estate brokerage with data, technology and transparency.
about.heroTaglineSub: No fluff. No improvisation. No empty promises.
about.introP1: Propyte is a real estate marketing firm that connects data, technology and human vision so that advisors, investors and developers make intelligent, reliable and strategic decisions. We operate in Playa del Carmen and Tulum, with planned expansion to Yucatan and Latin America.
about.introP2: Our name comes from Property + Byte: the union of real estate with data intelligence. We don't sell smoke or empty promises. We give every investor better tools, better market insight and a real advantage when making decisions.
about.missionText: We market residential and investment real estate developments, connecting advisors, investors and developers through clear processes, applied technology and strategic analysis, offering an efficient, transparent and results-oriented experience.
about.mission1Desc: We don't sell individual homes. We represent complete projects: residential, touristic and investment.
about.mission2Desc: Developer, investor and advisor. Three actors that historically operate disconnected. Propyte connects them.
about.mission3Desc: CRM, financial simulators, performance dashboards and real ROI analysis. Not intuition — data.
about.mission4Desc: If something doesn't add up, we say it. If a project isn't right for a certain profile, we point it out. Trust is built by telling the truth.
about.visionText: To be the most reliable, efficient and technologically advanced real estate marketer in Latin America, recognized for professionalizing brokerage through structure, data and a well-supported commercial talent ecosystem.
about.vision1Desc: When someone hears 'Propyte' they think: I can trust them.
about.vision2Desc: Technology so decisions are based on data, not gut feelings.
about.vision3Desc: Proof that there's another way to do real estate: with structure, training and standards.
about.valueTitle: Who do we create value for?
about.value1Desc: CRM, commercial materials, constant training and product analysis. You arrive prepared to every conversation — no need to improvise arguments.
about.value2Desc: Real numbers, logically projected scenarios, explained risks and end-to-end support. We don't sell a dream — we present an informed decision.
about.value3Desc: We understand your product, protect your brand, structure your commercial positioning and give real pipeline visibility. We're a strategic partner.
about.value4Desc: Clear commissions, updated inventory, professional materials and a process where you know exactly where you stand at all times.
about.culture1Desc: You don't advance by seniority or by being likeable. You advance because your results, discipline and professionalism demonstrate it consistently.
about.culture2Desc: Every data point has a source. Every recommendation has evidence. We don't use motivational language as a substitute for argument.
about.culture3Desc: If a project isn't right for a certain profile, we point it out. Trust is built by telling the truth, not what the other person wants to hear.
about.culture4Desc: CRM, simulators, dashboards — not to complicate, but so every conversation is backed by real information.
about.culture5Desc: Every process, every tool, every operational decision passes through a filter: could a new Team Leader execute this in another city with just the manual?
about.ctaTitle: Want to be part of it?
about.ctaSubtitle: We're looking for advisors, Team Leaders and commercial allies who share our philosophy.
about.structureSubtitle: Propyte operates as a commercial unit under a corporate structure that handles HR, finance, IT and back-office.
about.structurePhilosophyText: Propyte is organized into three directions —Commercial, Operations, and Finance— reporting to General Management. Each area has a clear owner, a measurable objective, and documented processes. The corporate functions (Technology, Human Resources, and Finance) provide cross-functional support so the commercial operation runs the same way in every location. This isn't a decorative org chart: it's the map of who answers for what.
about.structurePhilosophyHighlight: Every area has an owner, an objective, and a way to measure itself. That's how Propyte grows: with order, not improvisation.
about.teamSubtitle: Propyte advisors with deep knowledge of the Riviera Maya market.
about.teamIntro: Propyte's commercial team operates from the Real Estate Lab on 5th Avenue, Playa del Carmen, with coverage in Tulum through Nativa Tulum. Every advisor is registered with SEDETUS and trained under our Commercial Playbook.
about.teamMore: + 5 active advisors in Playa del Carmen and Tulum

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.summaryStr: The Propyte Index ranks each zone on a 0-100 scale using four weighted factors: Occupancy (30%), Rate Growth (25%), RevPAR (25%), and Competition (20%). Higher scores indicate stronger rental markets for short-term vacation rentals.
methodology.summaryLtr: Our traditional rental analysis aggregates data from 7 real estate sources covering 62+ cities in Mexico. Rents are normalized, filtered for outliers, and segmented by zone, property type, and bedrooms to provide accurate market benchmarks.
methodology.strFactorOccupancyDesc: Average annual occupancy rate relative to the market median. Zones with sustained high occupancy score better.
methodology.strFactorRateGrowthDesc: Year-over-year ADR (Average Daily Rate) growth. Captures zones with increasing pricing power.
methodology.strFactorRevparDesc: Revenue Per Available Room, combining occupancy and rate into a single metric of revenue potential.
methodology.strFactorCompetitionDesc: Inverse supply saturation score. Fewer active listings relative to demand results in a higher score.
methodology.strFooter: Data based on public comparables from real estate portals with monthly updates. Scores are recalculated on the 1st of each month.
methodology.ltrSource1: Inmuebles24 — Largest Mexican real estate portal
methodology.ltrStep1: Daily scraping of active rental listings across all sources
methodology.ltrStep2: Zone normalization using canonical zone mapping (150+ zones)
methodology.ltrStep3: Outlier removal: rentals below $5,000 or above $500,000 MXN/month excluded
methodology.ltrStep4: Statistical computation: median, average, percentiles by segment
methodology.ltrStep5: ML-based rent estimation for each development using comparable matching
methodology.ltrStep6: ROI, IRR, cap rate and cash flow projections computed per property
methodology.ltrFooter: Data is refreshed daily. Financial projections are estimates and do not guarantee returns.

faq.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know about buying, investing and renting property in Mexico.
faq.q1: Can foreigners buy property in Mexico?
faq.a1: Yes. Foreigners can buy property in Mexico. In restricted zones (50 km from coast, 100 km from borders), a bank trust (fideicomiso) is required, which grants you the same rights as a Mexican owner.
faq.a2: Closing costs vary by state: 6-8% of property value in Quintana Roo (Cancún, Tulum, Playa del Carmen) and 5-7% in Yucatán (Mérida). This includes acquisition tax, notary fees and registration fees.
faq.q3: How long does the buying process take?
faq.a3: The complete process takes 30-90 days depending on property type and financing. Cash purchases are faster (30-45 days). With a mortgage it can extend to 60-90 days.
faq.a4: A legal instrument where a Mexican bank holds the property on behalf of the foreign buyer. It grants full rights of use, rental, sale and inheritance. It renews automatically every 50 years.
faq.q5: What ROI can I expect in the Riviera Maya?
faq.a5: ROI varies by strategy: pre-sale appreciation 20-40% (2-3 years), residential rental 5-8% annually, and vacation rental 8-14% annually. We use public comparables from real estate portals and market benchmarks to estimate ranges. Ranges are referential, not guaranteed.
faq.q6: Is residential or vacation rental better?
faq.a6: It depends on your profile. Residential rental offers stable income with less management effort. Vacation rental generates more income but requires active management or a property manager (15-25% commission).
faq.a7: Cap Rate = (Annual Net Income / Purchase Price) × 100. It's the return rate if you buy in cash, without considering financing. A 6-8% Cap Rate is considered good for the Mexican market.
faq.q8: Can foreigners get a mortgage in Mexico?
faq.a8: Some Mexican banks offer mortgages to foreigners with FM2/FM3 (temporary or permanent residence). Requirements tend to be stricter and rates slightly higher than for nationals.
faq.a9: A payment plan where you pay the developer directly in monthly installments during construction, typically at 0% interest. Requires a 20-30% down payment with the balance covered in 12-36 months.
faq.q10: How much can I earn renting on Airbnb in Tulum?
faq.a10: A 1-bedroom apartment in Tulum can generate $15,000-$30,000 MXN/month in vacation rental at 60-75% occupancy. A 2-bedroom can reach $25,000-$50,000 MXN/month. Figures are referential and based on public comparables from real estate portals; actual returns vary by zone, management quality and season.
faq.q11: Do I need a property manager?
faq.a11: For vacation rental, it's highly recommended. A property manager handles check-in/check-out, cleaning, maintenance and guest communication. Typical commission is 15-25% of gross income.
faq.a12: No. Our service is free for buyers and investors. We work with developers who cover our fees as part of their sales strategy.
faq.q13: Where does the market data come from?
faq.a13: Our analyses combine public comparables from real estate portals (occupancy, ADR, RevPAR), rental benchmarks and proprietary internal models. Data is updated weekly.
faq.q14: How do I filter properties by my budget?
faq.a14: On /propiedades use the min/max price, city, property type and stage (preventa/construction/ready-to-deliver) filters. Results update in real time. You can save frequent searches in your profile.

comoInvertir.heroSubtitle: Three strategies to build wealth through real estate investment in Mexico. Each with its horizon, risk, and logic — so you choose on criteria, not hunches.
comoInvertir.strat1Desc: Buy in pre-construction at launch price and sell at delivery. In high-demand zones, value may appreciate during construction.
comoInvertir.strat2Desc: Acquire properties in zones with long-term rental demand. Stable monthly income with 6-12 month contracts. Lower risk, steady flow.
comoInvertir.strat3Desc: Short-term rental in tourist destinations. Higher potential return, but requires active management or a property manager. Performance depends on the zone, the season, and current regulation (RETUR-Q).
comoInvertir.strategiesFootnote: ROI by strategy is calculated with verifiable market data and shown as soon as the information is available. Actual returns vary by property, zone, and conditions at the time of investing. Past returns don't guarantee future results.

financiamiento.method1Desc: Traditional bank financing with rates from 9.5% annually. Requires 10-20% down payment, income verification, and good credit history. Terms of 10 to 20 years.
financiamiento.method1Ideal: Buyers with verifiable income and good credit score
financiamiento.method2Desc: Full payment at time of purchase. Eliminates interest and bank paperwork. Many developers offer 5-15% discounts for cash payment.
financiamiento.method2Ideal: Investors with available capital seeking the best price
financiamiento.method3Desc: Direct payment plans with the developer during construction phase. 20-30% down payment and monthly installments at 0% interest until delivery.
financiamiento.method3Ideal: Pre-sale purchases with monthly payment capacity
financiamiento.method4Desc: Temporary financing that allows you to buy a new property before selling your current one. Short terms of 6-12 months with higher rates.
financiamiento.method4Ideal: Those who want to buy before selling another property

glosario.metaDescription: Complete glossary with 22 real estate and investment terms: ROI, Cap Rate, IRR, Appreciation, Trust and more. Learn the language of Mexican real estate.

glosario.term1Def: Average vacation rental rate per night. Calculated by dividing total income by occupied nights.
glosario.term2Def: Shared facilities and services in a development: pool, gym, rooftop, security, coworking and more.
glosario.term3Def: Technical report that determines the commercial value of a property. Required by banks to grant credit and by notaries for title transfer.
glosario.term4Def: Annual net rental income divided by purchase price. A 6-8% Cap Rate is considered good in the Mexican market.
glosario.term5Def: Annual cash return on actual cash invested (down payment + closing costs), not considering appreciation.
glosario.term6Def: Temporary financing to buy a property before selling another. Short terms of 6-12 months.
glosario.term7Def: Ratio measuring whether rental income covers mortgage payment. DSCR > 1.25 is ideal for bank qualification.
glosario.term8Def: Initial payment when buying a property. In Mexico, typically 10-30% of value depending on financing type.
glosario.term9Def: Completed property ready to inhabit or rent, with no construction waiting period.
glosario.term10Def: Legal process of transferring property before a notary public. Costs include taxes, notary fees, and registration.
glosario.term11Def: Legal instrument allowing foreigners to "own" property in restricted zones of Mexico through a bank. Renews every 50 years.
glosario.term12Def: Annualized return considering the time value of money. Includes appreciation, rental income, and costs over time.
glosario.term13Def: Gross rental income minus operating expenses (maintenance, management, insurance). Does not include mortgage payment.
glosario.term14Def: Percentage of nights a vacation property is rented. In Riviera Maya, the average is 60-75%.
glosario.term15Def: Increase in property value over time. In high-demand zones like Tulum, it can be 10-20% annually.
glosario.term16Def: Stage where units are sold before construction begins. Offers the best prices (20-40% less than ready-to-move-in).
glosario.term17Def: Person or company managing a vacation rental: check-in/check-out, cleaning, maintenance. Typical commission: 15-25%.
glosario.term18Def: Revenue per available night = ADR × Occupancy Rate. Key metric for comparing performance between properties or zones.
glosario.term19Def: Total return (rental + appreciation) relative to amount invested, expressed as an annual percentage.
glosario.term20Def: Annual rental income as a percentage of property value. Gross yield = gross rent, Net yield = after expenses.
glosario.term21Def: Strip of 50 km from the coast and 100 km from Mexico's borders where foreigners need a trust (fideicomiso) to buy property.
glosario.term22Def: Commercial status of the development within the CRM. Defines whether the property is available, reserved, in process, or delivered.

unete.heroTitle: Your real estate career, with real backing
unete.heroTitlePart1: Your real estate career, with real
unete.heroSubtitle: At Propyte you don't sell alone. We assign you leads, give you technology and ongoing training, and back you with a team that wants you to close. Competitive commission, no desk fee, with a career path where you advance on results — not seniority.
unete.whyTitlePart1: You don't sell alone — you sell with a
unete.whySubtitle: The in-house advisor model: you close, and behind you there are leads, technology, training, and a team backing you on every deal.
unete.benefit1Desc: You don't start from zero or cold-prospect. You get leads from real buyers arriving in your CRM, ready to work.
unete.benefit2Desc: You earn on what you close, with a competitive commission. No desk rent, no fixed monthly charges.
unete.benefit3Desc: A weekly training program in product, fideicomiso, investment analysis, and closing. You learn while you sell, not before you start.
unete.benefit4Desc: CRM, financial simulators, e-signature, and real-time reports. Your whole business from your phone or laptop.
unete.benefit5Desc: From Junior Advisor to Team Leader, you advance on performance — not seniority or politics. You control how fast you climb.
unete.benefit6Desc: You operate under a brand with methodology, real support, and a team that's with you on every close. You're not alone in front of the client.
unete.careerIntro: At Propyte you don't advance by seniority or by being liked. You move up when your numbers prove it. Here's the path:
unete.careerLevel1Desc: Your entry point. Assigned leads, intensive training, and a mentor with you on your first closes. Ideal if you're just starting.
unete.careerLevel2Desc: You've mastered the process and close consistently. More autonomy, better leads, commission that grows with your performance.
unete.careerLevel3Desc: The top of the advisor track. You close high and steady. Recognition, priority on inventory, and the best commission terms.
unete.careerLevel4Desc: You lead your own team of advisors. Here the model shifts: base salary + commission, because you no longer just sell — you develop others who do.
unete.careerCallout: You start on commission, with leads and backing from day one. And if you build the path, you reach a leadership position with a fixed base. You decide how far.
unete.techSubtitle: Every Propyte advisor gets the full suite, built for the Mexican real estate market.
unete.tool1Desc: Lead tracking, sales pipeline, and automation.
unete.tool2Desc: Your advisor page with listings synced daily.
unete.tool4Desc: Conversion metrics, marketing ROI, and projections.
unete.tool5Desc: Email, social, and ads set up for you.
unete.tool6Desc: A dedicated team via WhatsApp, chat, and video call.
unete.step1Desc: Fill out the form with your experience. We reply within 24 hours.
unete.step2Desc: Meet your future Team Leader in a 30-minute video call.
unete.step3Desc: One week to master the tools, training, and your first portfolio.
unete.step4Desc: From day one: leads, portfolio, and all the tools.
unete.faq1Q: Do I have to leave my current agency to join?
unete.faq1A: For the in-house advisor role we look for dedication to the Propyte team. If what you have is your own client base and you'd rather keep your independence, our allied broker program may fit you better. We define which one suits you in the interview.
unete.faq2Q: Is there a base salary or commission only?
unete.faq2A: As an advisor (Junior, Senior, PRO-pyte Executive) you work on commission — but with assigned leads and no desk fee, so you don't start from zero. Once you reach Team Leader, the model shifts to base salary + commission.
unete.faq3A: You earn a percentage of the commission Propyte receives from the developer on each sale you close. The percentage is set by your level and agreed clearly — no hidden tiers or surprise charges.
unete.faq4Q: Do you give me leads or do I have to prospect?
unete.faq4A: We assign you leads from real buyers arriving in your CRM. You don't depend on cold prospecting to start producing.
unete.faq5Q: Do I need a license or prior real estate experience?
unete.faq5A: No. Many of our advisors started with none. That's what the Junior level is for: intensive training and a mentor with you through your first close.
unete.faq6A: We operate across the Riviera Maya (Playa del Carmen, Tulum, Cancún) and Yucatán (Mérida), with domestic and international buyers.
unete.faq7A: Your performance drives your progress on the career path, but we work with support, not arbitrary pressure. Expectations are set clearly from onboarding.
unete.faq8Q: What happens to my clients if I leave?
unete.faq8A: Clients and leads you work through Propyte are registered in the platform. Terms are set out clearly when you join — no surprises.
unete.otherBody: Propyte also grows in marketing, technology, operations, and administration. If you want to join a company that does things with data and without smoke —in a non-sales role— we'd like to meet you too.
